Merlin Kafka

📌 [...] Promoted

Merlin Kafka

📌

Hey Product Hunt, I'm Merlin, co-founder of Rex. Lewis and I spent four years building AR software at Sequence. What we kept seeing: someone on the finance team copying data from a portal into a spreadsheet into NetSuite, every single day. Automation tools never fixed it because the work is full of exceptions and judgment calls. So we built Rex to just do the work. Agents plug into your existing stack and run collections, portal uploads, and the AR inbox end to end. Anything sensitive gets routed to your team. Synthesia runs their AR on us today. Ask me anything, including what the agents still get wrong.

results.title: The Best AI Workflow Automation Tools
results.content:
Human checkpoints are treated as features, not compromises. Several reviews describe using Relay's AI agents for operational decision-making—routing edge cases, approving sensitive requests, and managing processes that can't just fire and forget. Relay makes workflows explainable to the people accountable for their outcomes.

> The value proposition is unbeatable. I was able to build a custom replacement for a specialized SaaS tool that was costing us double what we now pay for our entire Relay.app subscription. With just a Google Form, a single AI agent, and a few workflows, we replaced a manual intake process. It now runs in real time and handles edge cases automatically.

### Pros - Best use cases [...] The result is a curated list that reflects not just popularity, but genuine quality. If it's on this page, we'd recommend it to a friend.

## Frequently asked questions about AI Workflow Automation

Real answers from real users, pulled straight from launch discussions, forums, and reviews.

 ### Q: Can AI agent automation platforms run scheduled multi-step workflows across apps?

  Instruct can — these platforms routinely run scheduled, multi-step workflows across apps. [...] ### Pros - Best use cases

Ops and compliance teams running sensitive workflows who need visibility and built-in human approval steps.

### Cons - Watch out for

Smaller ecosystem. Less evidence of large-scale use. Not ideal for fire-and-forget automation.

### Standout features

 ✓Human-in-the-loop checkpoints built in
 ✓Full audit trail and execution visibility
 ✓AI agents for routing edge cases and approvals
 ✓Built for accountability, not just speed

### Pricing

 Free: 200 steps/month + 500 AI credits/month
 Professional: $19/month (billed annually)
 Team: $69/month (billed annually) · 10 users included
 Enterprise: custom

results.title: TableSprint: AI Platform to Build Future-Ready Apps | Product Hunt
results.content:
Review TableSprint?

Reviews

Most Informative

•2 reviews

🚀 I'm genuinely impressed! What caught my eye first was form builder with pre-build use case templates. I've always had to juggle between Airtable and Typeform for serious form needs, but TableSprint's form capabilities are surprisingly robust. What I appreciate most is how they've packaged everything: you can either build from scratch using their platform tools (AI writers, Kanban boards, etc.) or jump right in with pre-built solutions for common business processes like Order to Cash or Hire to Retire. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Highly recommended for teams looking to streamline their operations without breaking the bank. The only minor downside? You might spend too much time exploring all the features! 😄

Report

 11 views

Table Sprint

📌 [...] Report

 11 views

Table Sprint

📌

Great points in this post! I'd also add that tools like TableSprint are worth checking out if you want more than just workflow automation — it combines an AI agent builder with a real database, dashboards, and admin panels in one platform. Handy for teams that want to launch a working app or agent quickly without stitching together five different tools. 

Report

Necla Sülünbiye

A solid take for non-devs who live in spreadsheets. One thing I'd love to see is a proper public API or webhook for each app, so data changes in TableSprint can push updates into tools like Slack, CRMs, or accounting software. That would turn these apps into real workflow hubs instead of isolated trackers.

Report

Ufuk [...] ## Table sprint

Launched this week

software

TableSprint is an AI-first, no-code platform for building complete apps and agents, not just workflows. It offers ready-made AI agents for support, sales, and finance, a built-in Smart Database with dashboards, SOC 2 Type 2 security, audit trails, and easy Excel import/export. Teams have replaced multiple tools and launched full systems in weeks. A free plan lets you try it before upgrading. Explore TableSprint's agents and pricing on its website.
